verdict.summary: Guide is mostly solid on the abolition of Manifestação de Interesse and the 88/89 distinction, but it contains a major incorrect future/nationality-law claim, several over-broad statements, and some document requirements are presented as mandatory when they are not fixed legal requirements.

issues.current: Натурализация: 5 лет по действующему закону; **03.05.2026 Президент Сегуру промульгировал Decreto AR 48/XVII** — после публикации в DRE будет 7 лет для CPLP+EU / 10 лет для остальных
issues.problem: As of April 2026 this is factually wrong / premature. The nationality-law amendment was approved on 01.04.2026 and was awaiting promulgation; it should not be stated as already promulgated on 03.05.2026 or as certain to enter into force. The guide also has no source for this sensitive claim.
issues.suggestion: Write instead: "Натурализация: по действующему закону — 5 лет легального проживания. 01.04.2026 парламент одобрил изменения к Lei da Nacionalidade, предлагающие увеличить срок до 7 лет для граждан CPLP/ЕС и до 10 лет для остальных, но на дату проверки закон ожидал промульгации/публикации и ещё не вступил в силу. Перед подачей проверяйте DRE и IRN."
issues.source: New nationality law approved 01.04.2026 awaiting promulgation; Lei da Nacionalidade / DRE status as of April 2026

issues.problem: The guide is being assessed as of April 2026, but the metadata claims verification on 17.05.2026 and the text relies on a supposed event of 03.05.2026. This creates an internal chronology problem and makes the article look forward-dated.
issues.suggestion: Set lastVerified to the actual review date in April 2026, or remove post-April events. If the article is intended to be current after May 2026, re-check and source every post-April claim.

issues.current: ❌ Въехавшим после 03.06.2024 — только через визу D в консульстве
issues.problem: Too broad. For work/self-employment under old 88.2/89.2 MdI this is essentially true, but as written it sounds like every post-03.06.2024 foreigner has no in-country route at all. Exceptions/other regimes can exist, e.g. CPLP for eligible nationals, family reunification, EU-family-member routes, protection/asylum, and other special legal categories.
issues.suggestion: Change to: "Для новых заявок именно по старым маршрутам 88.2/89.2 после 03.06.2024 путь MdI закрыт; стандартный путь для работы/самозанятости — резидентская виза D в консульстве. Другие режимы, например CPLP, reagrupamento familiar, EU-family-member и защита/убежище, регулируются отдельно."

issues.current: Доказательство регулярного дохода (банковские выписки, Recibo Verde за ≥ 6 мес)
issues.problem: Misleading as a standard requirement for 89.1. A fixed "Recibo Verde за ≥ 6 мес" requirement is not a general statutory rule and can be impossible for someone entering via D2 and then applying for the first residence permit. AIMA may ask for evidence of real activity/income, contracts, invoices, start-of-activity declaration, tax/SS compliance, etc., but not necessarily six months of recibos verdes.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Доказательство реальной деятельности и средств: declaração de início de atividade, contracts/propostas de prestação de serviços, recibos verdes/faturas если уже есть, банковские выписки, налоговые/SS-документы. Требования зависят от типа деятельности и запроса AIMA; фиксированного правила ‘6 месяцев recibos verdes’ нет."
issues.source: Lei 23/2007 art. 89; AIMA residence-permit document lists

issues.problem: Misleading limitation. NISS is not only relevant to employees; independent professionals under art. 89 usually also need Segurança Social registration/compliance once activity is open and contributions are due. The guide itself later asks for active NISS and paid contributions for 89.1, so this is internally inconsistent.
issues.suggestion: Change to: "NISS / регистрация в Segurança Social — для наёмных работников и, как правило, для independent professionals после открытия деятельности; подтверждение актуальной ситуации/взносов по запросу AIMA."
issues.source: Segurança Social rules for trabalhadores independentes; AIMA practice for arts. 88/89

issues.current: Регистрация деятельности в Finanças (CAE, соответствующий вашей деятельности)
issues.problem: For an individual trabalhador independente / recibos verdes the activity is often registered under CIRS activity codes, not only CAE. CAE is relevant especially for companies and some business activities. Saying only CAE can confuse freelancers.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Регистрация начала деятельности в Finanças с подходящим CIRS-кодом и/или CAE, в зависимости от формата деятельности."
issues.source: Autoridade Tributária rules on início de atividade

issues.current: 📄 Atestado de Residência (свидетельство о месте жительства) от Junta de Freguesia
issues.problem: Presented as universally mandatory. AIMA generally requires proof of accommodation/address; a Junta atestado is one common form, but not always the only acceptable document. Lease contract, property deed, invitation/term of responsibility or other proof may be accepted depending on the case.
issues.suggestion: Change to: "Подтверждение адреса/жилья: договор аренды, собственность, atestado de residência от Junta de Freguesia или иной документ, который AIMA принимает в вашем случае."
issues.source: AIMA document lists for residence permits

issues.current: 📄 Апостилированная справка о несудимости страны происхождения
issues.problem: Incomplete. Criminal-record certificates are normally required from the country of nationality and/or countries of residence for more than one year, depending on the procedure, and must be valid, legalized/apostilled where applicable and translated if needed. "страны происхождения" is vague and may not be enough.
issues.suggestion: Write: "Справка о несудимости из страны гражданства и, если применимо, из стран, где вы проживали более 1 года; документ должен быть действителен, легализован/апостилирован при необходимости и переведён на португальский, если AIMA требует."
issues.source: AIMA residence-permit document lists; Lei 23/2007 general criminal-record requirements

issues.current: После 5 лет легального проживания — право на постоянный ВНЖ
issues.problem: Too automatic. Permanent residence after five years is subject to additional conditions, including valid temporary residence history and statutory requirements such as means, accommodation, Portuguese-language knowledge and absence of disqualifying criminal issues.
issues.suggestion: Write: "После 5 лет временного ВНЖ можно просить постоянный ВНЖ при выполнении условий закона: средства, жильё, базовый португальский, отсутствие препятствующих судимостей и др."

issues.current: ⏳ AIMA постепенно обрабатывает накопившиеся MdI — приёмы идут до 2027 г.
issues.problem: Potentially misleading if stated as a firm official timeline. AIMA backlog processing is ongoing, but individual scheduling is unpredictable and there is no guaranteed universal end-date for all MdI cases.
issues.suggestion: Change to: "AIMA продолжает поэтапно обрабатывать backlog MdI; сроки зависят от дела и региона, гарантированного максимального срока или даты завершения для всех дел нет."
issues.source: AIMA public communications / backlog processing practice

missing_topics: Add an explicit government/source citation for the nationality-law status; this is a high-risk topic and should not rely on an unsourced sentence.

Mention transitional-processing caveats for pre-03.06.2024 MdI cases: applicants must react to AIMA notifications within the stated deadline and keep documents updated; pending MdI is not a residence card.
Add direct AIMA links to the current document checklists for arts. 88 and 89 rather than only the AIMA homepage.
Clarify that document requirements can differ between consular visa stage and AIMA residence-permit stage; some documents submitted for the visa may still be requested again or updated by AIMA.
